perturbation.original_text: Distributor shall use its best efforts to market, promote and sell the Products to the authorized customers in the Filed of Use and in the Territory during the term of this agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: Distributor is encouraged but not required to market, promote and sell the Products to the authorized customers in the Filed of Use and in the Territory during the term of this agreement.
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'Distributor shall use its best efforts' to 'Distributor is encouraged but not required,' the obligation to actively market and sell the products becomes discretionary. This contradicts the implicit expectation elsewhere in the contract, specifically Exhibit C and section 6a, that the distributor will meet minimum performance goals and that the agreement will have an initial term of 3 years and renew for 3 years with 'new minimum performance goals'. The contradiction arises because a discretionary marketing effort makes the attainment of any performance goals uncertain. If performance goals are not met, there would be contradiction regarding the performance goals and the auto renewal based on them.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Exhibit C Minimum Performance Goals Minimum Performance won't be determined until FDA 50k market clearance is obtained. At that time parties will operate in good faith to set performance goals. Note: Performance goals to begin 120 days after receipt of FDA market clearance.
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces a 'best efforts' obligation with a discretionary action ('encouraged but not required'). This directly contradicts Exhibit C, which anticipates 'minimum performance goals' being set. The discretionary nature of the distributor's effort now makes the setting and attainment of performance goals uncertain, creating a strong inconsistency that could render Exhibit C unenforceable. If the distributor is not *required* to market, promote, and sell the product, then *minimum performance goals* become logically inconsistent and contradict the expectations in the contradicted text.

perturbation.original_text: Erchonia agrees to provide Products to Distributor pursuant to orders placed by Distributor in the form of individual purchase orders issued by Distributor to Erchonia. Such purchase orders shall set forth the quantity of each product ordered, the required delivery date, and the point of delivery.
perturbation.changed_text: Erchonia will attempt to provide Products to Distributor pursuant to orders placed by Distributor in the form of individual purchase orders issued by Distributor to Erchonia. Such purchase orders may set forth the quantity of each product ordered, the requested delivery date, and the suggested point of delivery.
perturbation.explanation: By modifying 'Erchonia agrees to provide' to 'Erchonia will attempt to provide' and changing 'shall set forth' to 'may set forth', we are weakening Erchonia's obligation to fulfill orders and making the details within the purchase orders optional. This introduces contradiction, because a purchase order is a binding document. This directly contradicts the section 6e which expects Erchonia will complete delivery of all purchase orders accepted by it prior to termination, because if the orders are not binding, or are not fully detailed this creates an internal contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Except upon termination due to Distributors breach of this agreement, Erchonia will complete delivery of all purchase orders accepted by it prior to termination of this agreement; provided that Distributor shall remain liable for full payment of all such orders.
perturbation.justification: The modified text weakens Erchonia's obligation to provide products and makes the details within the purchase orders optional, by changing 'Erchonia agrees to provide' to 'Erchonia will attempt to provide' and changing 'shall set forth' to 'may set forth'. This contradicts the contradicted text, which states "Erchonia will complete delivery of all purchase orders accepted by it prior to termination of this agreement," because if the orders are not binding or fully detailed, there's no clear basis to enforce Erchonia's delivery obligation. The purchase orders now lack the binding details, so the delivery of all purchase orders accepted creates an internal contradiction due to ambiguity.

perturbation.original_text: Upon Erchonia's request, the Distributor shall at its expense deliver to Erchonia representative samples of labels, advertisements, catalogs, spec sheets, and the like, containing the Trademarks, and to inspect all of Distributors websites, social media accounts and any online information posted by Distributor, to ensure that such Trademarks are used only in a manner complaint with the quality standards. Should such material fail to meet the standards set by Erchonia, as determined by Erchonia in its sole discretion, Distributor shall not use and shall withdraw and retract any promotion or advertising that Erchonia finds unsuitable and will at its expense destroy such materials or make them compliant.
perturbation.changed_text: Upon Erchonia's polite request, the Distributor may at its convenience deliver to Erchonia representative samples of labels, advertisements, catalogs, spec sheets, and the like, containing the Trademarks, and allow Erchonia to inspect all of Distributors websites, social media accounts and any online information posted by Distributor, to see if such Trademarks are used only in a manner compliant with the quality standards. Should such material seem to potentially fail to meet the standards set by Erchonia, as maybe determined by Erchonia, Distributor might consider withdrawing and retracting any promotion or advertising that Erchonia finds unsuitable and possibly destroying such materials or making them compliant.
perturbation.explanation: Changing the tone and obligation of trademark compliance from mandatory to optional introduces ambiguity and contradiction. The original text mandates the distributor's actions to ensure trademark compliance. The modified text uses softer language, making the distributor's actions discretionary. This new version creates an internal contradiction with section 9(a) which states 'All trademarks...are and remain the exclusive property of Erchonia.' If the distributor's compliance with trademark standards is optional, Erchonia's exclusive property rights are not adequately protected.
perturbation.contradicted_text: All trademarks, including service marks, trade names and trade address that Erchonia uses in connection with the license granted hereunder (the "Trademarks") are and remain the exclusive property of Erchonia. Nothing contained in this Agreement shall be deemed to give Distributor any right, title or interest in any Trademark. The Trademarks include but are not limited to: the names "Erchonia", "Erchonia Medical", the phrase "Erchonia, Designs Into the Future", the Erchonia logo, packaging design, and Product design.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the Distributor's obligations regarding trademark compliance from mandatory to discretionary by using softer language like 'may' and 'might'. This directly contradicts the original intent of the agreement as stated in the Contradicted Text: 'All trademarks...are and remain the exclusive property of Erchonia'. If the Distributor's actions are optional, Erchonia's exclusive property rights are not adequately protected, which creates a direct contradiction and enforceability concern. The original text ensures stricter enforcement of Erchonia's trademark rights, whereas the changed text significantly weakens those rights, creating a clear contradiction.
